page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying x86_64 architecture supports instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI, but lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the top tier for PassMark memory performance and LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model. It demonstrates strong performance in single-core CPU stress tests, image processing, encryption, and physics simulations, as well as medium-model LLM text generation. Most other metrics, including multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput, fall within the average range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per vCPU makes this instance suitable for memory-bound workloads without requiring the purchase of excess compute capacity. However, the lack of local SSD storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which introduces a potential I/O bottleneck. The absence of GPU acceleration also limits its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale deep learning training.

This hardware configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, caching layers, medium-scale LLM inference, and data processing pipelines. It is also appropriate for enterprise applications, software compilation, and web serving workloads that require a large memory footprint but moderate CPU compute power.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 16 physical cores, it provides 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, it achieves top-tier performance in PassMark memory tests and small-model LLM text generation, alongside strong single-core CPU and encryption capabilities. Most multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks align with average mid-range performance. This configuration offers high memory density, making it cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, medium-scale LLM inference, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering top-tier memory performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-32 features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, delivering top-tier memory performance.
tagline: A memory-dense, AMD-powered virtual server delivering top-tier memory performance and balanced compute for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor.
Top-tier PassMark memory score and small-model LLM text generation speed.
Strong single-core CPU performance, encryption, and physics simulation capabilities.
No local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage.
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and medium-scale LLM inference.
